title: Ibidium beckii
intro: Ibidium beckii, also known as Ilex beckii, is a species of plant in the family Aquifoliaceae, commonly referred to as the holly family, and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Ilex beckii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ilex beckii is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Ibidium beckii is a species of beetle within the family Staphylinidae, which includes the rove beetles. This family is part of the larger superfamily Staphylinoidea and the order Coleoptera. The order Coleoptera is a group of beetles that also includes the weevils, scarab beetles, and longhorned beetles, among others. The superfamily Staphylinoidea is a subgroup of the larger infraorder Staphyliniformia, which also includes the family Silvanidae. The infraorder Staphyliniformia is part of the order Coleoptera.
